weather.summary: Saturday: 9°C, light rain. Sunday: 13°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend offers a mix of indoor fun perfect for the drizzly weather! I've found some great options for your little one, from creative workshops at the Southbank Centre to exploring the Young V&A and enjoying puppet shows. Let's make the most of the weekend with these engaging and enriching activities!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Formerly the V&A Museum of Childhood, this newly renovated museum is designed to spark creativity in children. Explore collections of toys, games and design with hands-on exhibits and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers sensory play areas and interactive exhibits perfect for toddlers. Plenty of space to move around and explore. Free entry is a bonus!

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line from Bethnal Green station. It's a short walk from the station to the museum.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for daily drop-in activities.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the vast parklands, playgrounds and walking trails. Check out the Tumbling Bay playground for natural play or the Pleasure Gardens fountains. Some areas may have specific opening times.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers space to run around, various playgrounds suitable for different ages, and sensory experiences with water features. Great for burning energy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford. The park is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for events happening this weekend.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small but charming city farm with a variety of animals, including sheep, goats, chickens and rabbits. There is also a garden and an excellent on-site Italian cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see and interact with animals, which is great for toddlers. Small and manageable size. The cafe provides a good option for lunch or a snack.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or cycle. It's centrally located in Hackney.
transport.tip: Check for special events or feeding times.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Step into historical homes through the ages! See how people lived through different eras, with a beautiful enclosed garden. Pick up an activity sheet for kids at the front desk.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a glimpse into different eras with interesting room setups. The garden offers a nice outdoor space.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or Overground to Hoxton. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Check for family events or workshops on their website.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a pond theme, perfect for toddlers. Located within the Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for toddlers, with a safe and engaging play environment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford. The park is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the larger playgrounds in the park.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: One of the oldest city farms in London, home to a range of animals and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Small and manageable for toddlers, offering a chance to see farm animals and learn about gardening.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or Overground to Shoreditch High Street. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops.

activities.title: Hackney Downs Adventure Playground
activities.description: A staffed adventure playground with a range of play equipment and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and engaging environment for children to play and explore, especially with adult supervision for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or cycle.
transport.tip: Check the website for opening times and any specific age restrictions.

activities.title: Tramshed
activities.description: A theatre and arts venue offering a range of family-friendly shows and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of performances suitable for young children, providing a cultural experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Woolwich.
transport.tip: Check the website for the schedule and book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A riverside hub of arts, fun, and food. Enjoy free installations, creative workshops, and open spaces to roam. Check for family-friendly events and performances.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of open space for scooting/running, plus family-friendly activities and performances. Many free options.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Short walk to Southbank Centre.
transport.tip: Check the website for the weekend's schedule of free events.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur skeletons, a blue whale model, and interactive exhibits. A classic London museum experience for families.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs are a hit with toddlers! Lots to see and discover, with free admission.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid long queues, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover interactive exhibits, space exploration galleries, and flight simulators. Great for hands-on learning.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers lots of interactive exhibits to engage toddlers. Wonderlab is a great hands-on experience (ticketed).

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore Egyptian mummies, Greek sculptures, and ancient artifacts. A cultural and educational experience for the whole family.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see fascinating artifacts from around the world. Activity sheets and Explorer Backpacks available at the family desk.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then the Central Line to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road.
transport.tip: Book a free timed ticket in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals, including wallabies and lemurs. The park also has swimming ponds and a lido.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a variety of animals to see. A good option for a shorter outing.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the Overground to Hampstead Heath, then a bus to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A popular playground with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, a sensory trail, and teepees.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of play areas to suit different ages and interests. The pirate ship is a big draw for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Notting Hill Gate.
transport.tip: Can get very busy, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Features natural history exhibits, an aquarium, and beautiful gardens with views over London. There are also farm animals and a music laboratory for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities, with animals, gardens, and interactive exhibits. Perfect for a full day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops on their website.

activities.title: The Lion Inside
activities.description: A stage adaptation of the popular children's book about courage and confidence.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging story and suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to London Bridge.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials, offering a unique and sustainable play experience.
activities.whyItWorks: Encourages creativity and imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Short walk to Southbank Centre.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Easter Egg hunt, farm animals, indoor playbarn, outdoor adventure play villages.
activities.whyItWorks: Wide range of activities to keep children entertained for hours.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a fun Easter-themed activity.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Animal encounters combined with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Family Sundays at the Royal Opera House
activities.description: Fun, relaxed and creative sessions perfect for families with children aged 3 to 10.
activities.whyItWorks: Introduces children to the world of opera in a fun and accessible way.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: A musical adaptation of the beloved Paddington Bear stories.
activities.whyItWorks: Charming story and engaging music make it suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Covent Garden or Charing Cross.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
activities.description: A Shakespeare play adapted for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Introduces children to classic literature in an accessible way.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to London Bridge.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.
